revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂見神社 (沼津市根古屋)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Homiji Shrine | image = File:Homi-Jinjya.jpg | location = 359 Negoya Kojō, {{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}}, Shizuoka Prefecture | founded = 4th year of Ansei | location = {{ill|Numazu|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.141398|N|138.807057|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Homiji Shrine''' (穂見神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Negoya, {{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}} in Shizuoka Prefecture ({{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}).
== Overview == On the area beneath the {{ill|Main Tower|ja|Tenshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Tower|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the honmaru (main enclosure) on the north side of the former {{ill|Kōkokuji Castle|en|Kōkokuji Castle|qid=Q10855360|lt=Kokokuji Castle|1=Kōkokuji Castle}} ruins, there stands Homiji Shrine. To its right are monuments dedicated to {{ill|Hōjō Sōun|en|Hōjō Sōun|qid=Q924929|lt=Hojo Soun|1=Hōjō Sōun}} and {{ill|Amano Yasukage|en|Amano Yasukage|qid=Q4352847|lt=Amano Yasukage|1=Amano Yasukage}}. The plaque reads “Ansei 4, Mishi New Year, offering petition to Takao Daimyojin.”
According to tradition, in the Mishi New Year of the 4th year of Ansei, 15 patrons separated from the head shrine in Takao, located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, namely {{ill|Homiji Shrine (Minami Alps City)|ja|穂見神社 (南アルプス市)|lt=Takao-san Homiji Shrine|lt_ja=「高尾山穂見神社」|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homiji Shrine (Minami Alps City)|1=Homi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597095}}, to establish their own place of worship. It is said that following the occurrence of the {{ill|Ansei great earthquakes|en|Ansei great earthquakes|qid=Q849463|lt=The Ansei Great Earthquake|1=Ansei great earthquakes}} and subsequent salt damage from a large tsunami which led to consecutive poor harvests, the agricultural deity “Takao-san Homiji Shrine” was established in hopes of abundant harvests.<ref>[https://artscouncil-shizuoka.jp/culture-resource-db/detail.php?id=878 Homiji Shrine (Kokokuji Castle Ruins)] Fujinokuni Cultural Heritage Database, accessed April 15, 2018</ref>
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|Takao-san Kofun|ja|高尾山古墳|1=Takaozan Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao-san Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaozan Kofun|qq=}} – Located in the same city, this site is also associated with Takao-san Homiji Shrine. * {{ill|Kōkokuji Castle|en|Kōkokuji Castle|qid=Q10855360|lt=Kokokuji Castle|1=Kōkokuji Castle}} – Homiji Shrine has been enshrined since the 4th year of Ansei.
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|穂見神社 (沼津市根古屋)|version=97816894|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:穂見神社 (沼津市根古屋)]]
